events.label: reasoning
events.text: We need answer JSON only. Need evaluate scenario: EV battery service photo shows orange service disconnect removed and placed on cowl. Technician immediately begins removing inverter cover. Class 0 gloves test date expired nine months ago. No meter verification of absence of voltage shown. Need decision fail because dangerous/non-compliant. Risk high/critical? High voltage exposure, no PPE verification, no absence of voltage verification. Probably critical due to electrocution risk. Work stage in-progress. Component conditions: gloves non-compliant (expired test), maybe service disconnect removed is okay? The inverter cover removal before verifying isolation and voltage absence is unsafe. Conditions: non-compliant for gloves. Findings: service disconnect removed does not prove absence of voltage; inverter cover removed before lockout/tagout and verification; expired Class 0 gloves not rated/tested; no HV meter verification. Actions: stop work; don proper tested Class 0 gloves with current date; perform lockout/tagout; verify absence of voltage with HV meter at test points; follow OEM HV disable procedure; reinspect PPE. Rationale. References: OSHA 1910.333, NFPA 70E, OEM HV service procedures, maybe SAE J2990. Need include all fields. value 0, sound_source empty string? Schema says string. event_time 0.0, rate 0.0, order empty list, workable empty list, percent_complete maybe 10. confidence 1.0. Ensure valid JSON.

events.label: response
events.text:
{
  "decision": "fail",
  "risk": "critical",
  "work_stage": "in-progress",
  "component_conditions": ["non-compliant"],
  "percent_complete": 10,
  "value": 0,
  "sound_source": "",
  "confidence": 1.0,
  "event_time": 0.0,
  "rate": 0.0,
  "order": [],
  "workable": [],
  "findings": [
    "Service disconnect removed but no lockout/tagout or verification of absence of voltage performed before opening the inverter cover",
    "Class 0 gloves in use have an expired test date (nine months overdue), so their dielectric integrity is unverified and non-compliant",
    "No high-voltage meter verification of zero energy state is shown at the inverter or battery test points",
    "Removing a high-voltage enclosure cover without confirmed isolation creates direct electrocution exposure"
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Stop work immediately and secure the work area",
    "Follow OEM high-voltage disable and isolation procedure, including lockout/tagout of the service disconnect",
    "Verify absence of voltage with an approved high-voltage meter at designated test points before removing any HV cover",
    "Replace the expired Class 0 gloves with currently tested and certified HV-rated gloves; inspect for damage before use",
    "Use appropriate HV safety barriers, warning signage, and qualified-person protocols before resuming"
  ],
  "rationale": "Removing an inverter cover without verified zero-energy state and while using expired insulating gloves creates a critical shock/electrocution hazard. The service disconnect alone does not prove absence of voltage; OEM procedures require lockout/tagout and meter verification before exposing HV components.",
  "references": [
    "OEM HV service procedures",
    "OSHA 29 CFR 1910.333 - Selection and use of work practices",
    "NFPA 70E - Electrical Safety in the Workplace",
    "SAE J2990 - Hybrid and EV First and Second Responder Recommended Practice"
  ]
}
